title: cayratia cumingiana
intro: Cayratia cumingiana, also known as the Philippine grape ivy or climbing grape ivy, is a perennial climber vine in the family Vitaceae. It is traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Cayratia cumingiana include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate skin conditions, and treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in tea form, and it can also be used to make a topical paste to treat skin conditions and wounds.
